verdict_reasoning: HSPA5/GRP78/BiP is canonically an ER-luminal chaperone, but it is well-documented to translocate to the cell surface under stress and oncogenic transformation. On cancer cells it appears as a surface-exposed protein (ecto-GRP78) accessible to antibodies, ADCs, and CAR-T constructs without membrane permeabilization. This cancer-cell-restricted surface pool is the basis of multiple active clinical programs targeting cell-surface GRP78. It therefore qualifies as contextual/cell_state_induced despite its dominant ER-luminal localization at baseline.
